title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged theft of trade secrets claims company mentored incoming employees on bringing confidential information
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI and former employees over alleged trade secret theft. The lawsuit claims OpenAI mentored new employees on bringing confidential information from Apple. This dispute involves the development of AI hardware businesses.
insight: Confidentiality breaches threaten AI innovation and corporate competitive advantage

title: SK hynix and TetraMem collaborate on experimental chip to bolster energy efficiency for edge AI devices memristor-based in-memory SoC research leaves performance questions up in the air
summary: SK hynix, TetraMem, and USC collaborated on a memristor-based in-memory SoC, achieving promising energy efficiency for edge AI devices, but performance limitations remain.
insight: Energy efficiency breakthroughs in AI edge devices are crucial for widespread adoption.

title: Anthropic says it can read Claude's 'thoughts,' as detailed in new research paper models observed to have a global workspace, revealing more of what makes LLMs tick
summary: Anthropic's research paper reveals a 'J-space' in Claude AI, similar to human processing, which may aid in improving LLM honesty and oversight.
insight: Unlocking AI's internal workings for more transparent and accountable LLMs.

title: Steam sales reportedly topped $11 billion during H1 2026 due to shifting trends staggering growth driven by influx of Chinese players and booming legacy catalogues
summary: Steam generated an estimated $11.1 billion in revenue in H1 2026, surpassing the entire year of 2020 and outpacing last year's holiday season. This makes it the best six-month period in the platform's history. The growth is attributed to an influx of Chinese players and a thriving legacy catalogue.
insight: Shifting trends drive staggering growth on Steam due to Chinese players.

title: Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026) Review: Stunning Mini LED, serious muscle, and a few missed steps
summary: The Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 features an 18-inch mini-LED display and cutting-edge components, but falls short in certain areas. It offers impressive performance, but lacks PCIe 5.0 storage and dual-channel RAM. A comparison to the Razer Blade 18 reveals some performance differences.
insight: Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 meets expectations, but lacks key upgrades.

title: SK hynix raises a record $26.5 billion in historic U.S. IPO South Korean memory giant to fund massive HBM manufacturing expansions
summary: SK hynix raised a record $26.5 billion in its Nasdaq IPO, planning to invest in massive HBM manufacturing expansions. The funds will be used to meet surging AI demand and sold-out HBM supply. This historic IPO is a significant milestone for the South Korean memory giant.
insight: Funding AI-driven demand and supply chain expansion in memory market

title: Nanya to quadruple capital spending to $6.2 billion in 2027 as DRAM prices push gross margin to 79.5% Q2 revenue skyrockets as ASPs for memory continue to surge
summary: Nanya Technology plans to quadruple its capital spending to $6.2 billion in 2027. This significant increase in capital expenditure is largely driven by rising DRAM prices. The surge in DRAM prices has led to a substantial boost in the company's revenue and gross margin.
insight: DRAM price surge fuels Nanya's aggressive capital spending plans.

title: Japanese chipmaker Rapidus to offer lower wafer pricing than TSMC 2nm class silicon to be priced around $20,000 on 2027 launch
summary: Japanese chipmaker Rapidus plans to undercut TSMC's 2nm wafer pricing at launch in 2027, offering lower quotes.
insight: Industry shake-up due to aggressive pricing strategy

title: Save 84% on a two-year ExpressVPN subscription, offering four additional months for free upgrade your privacy for under $70 with no-logs access to servers in 105 countries worldwide
summary: Save $378 on a two-year ExpressVPN subscription, now priced at $69.72, with four extra months of free access to servers in 105 countries.
insight: Uphold online anonymity with secure, no-logs VPN service worldwide.

title: Researchers turn HBM on its side to tackle AI memory s heat wall Korean V-Die and Japanese MOSAIC designs promise higher bandwidth, denser stacks, and cooler future GPUs
summary: Researchers in Korea and Japan have proposed innovative sideways-stacked DRAM designs, overcoming conventional HBM limitations by improving cooling, bandwidth, and capacity, and reducing reliance on TSV-heavy vertical stacks. These designs aim to push future AI memory beyond its current capabilities. This could lead to more efficient and powerful AI systems.
insight: Innovative sideways stacks boost AI memory efficiency and performance.

title: Lua-programmable ELM11-Feather board puts Gowin GW1NR FPGA into Feather form factor (Crowdfunding)
summary: The ELM11-Feather board is a Lua-programmable microcontroller featuring a Gowin GW1NR FPGA in the Adafruit Feather form factor. It offers GPIO headers, a built-in debugger, and USB-C power and programming. The board can run a PicoRV32 MCU softcore at up to 75 MHz.
insight: Lua-programmability on an FPGA unlocks new microcontroller applications and flexibility.

title: Best of Sensors: Woman of the Year Tianyue Yu
summary: Tianyue Yu is recognized for her work as technologist and founder, spanning over two decades. She has made significant contributions to the field of sensors. Her dedication and expertise have earned her the title of Woman of the Year.
insight: Pioneering female technologists inspire future generations of innovators

title: Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging former employees stole trade secrets to build AI hardware
summary: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging former employees stole trade secrets for AI development.
insight: Trade secret theft can undermine intellectual property and competitive advantage.

title: Steam now has 50% more users than PlayStation, and Sony's own decisions might be pushing more players there
summary: Steam has surpassed PlayStation's user base, with a massive pool of potential customers. This shift could impact Sony's gaming strategy and revenue. Shawn Layden's comments on PC game releases take on new significance.
insight: Sony's PC gaming decisions might be driven by business considerations

title: Federal Reserve taps Xbox CEO Asha Sharma to lead jobs task force days after Microsoft laid off 3,200 people
summary: Federal Reserve appoints Xbox CEO Asha Sharma to lead a jobs task force, following Microsoft's mass layoffs.
insight: Corporate leaders' perspectives inform monetary policy decision-making processes.

title: Nvidia's rumored GeForce RTX 5090 SE promises flagship power for $500 less, if you can find one
summary: Nvidia's GeForce RTX 5090 SE reportedly features a cut-down version of the RTX 5090's silicon, with 14,080 CUDA cores and a lower price point of $500 less than the flagship model.
insight: Potential flagship performance at an affordable price is on the horizon.

title: SK Hynix raises $26.5 billion in massive US share offering
summary: SK Hynix has raised $26.5 billion in the largest US listing by a foreign company. This significant fundraising effort will likely impact the global semiconductor industry. The company's move is a testament to its growth and financial strength.
insight: Massive fundraising boosts SK Hynix's global semiconductor market presence.

title: Ubisoft defends $85 day-one DLC after AC Black Flag Resynced gets review-bombed
summary: Ubisoft's Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced was review-bombed due to $85 day-one DLC, surpassing game cost.
insight: Consumer trust eroded by excessive, pricey day-one DLC.

title: Data shows physical PlayStation games can be up to 90% cheaper than their digital versions
summary: Physical PlayStation games can be up to 90% cheaper than digital versions.
insight: Physical copies offer significant cost savings over digital alternatives.

title: FCC approves giant mirror satellite designed to beam sunlight to Earth after dark
summary: The FCC has granted permission for the E rendil-1 satellite to operate, allowing it to test redirecting sunlight onto Earth after dark.
insight: Potential game-changer for renewable energy and disaster relief efforts worldwide.

title: The EU just revived a law that lets Meta and Google scan your messages critics call it mass surveillance
summary: The EU has reinstated a law allowing Google and Meta to scan messages for illegal content.
insight: Mass surveillance risks infringing on users' right to privacy.
